sourceTitle: Generative AI Hub | Teaching & Learning — UCL
normalizedText:
Microsoft Copilot is our approved GenAI tool because of its enhanced data protection. This tool is a more secure and safer alternative to other GenAI services. Read about how you can use Microsoft Copilot easily and safely.

Staff can explore the GenAI Training SharePoint site for information about and recordings of sessions on GenAI, as well as links to further resources.

UCL offers a service called Studiosity to support students with their academic writing and referencing skills. The platform uses GenAI and is available 24/7 to all current students, at all levels of study. Staff can explore the Studiosity Resource Centre for support resources including How To guides, frequently asked questions and use cases.

For students

Learn about engaging with GenAI in education and assessments
Find out more about Studiosity and how to access the service
Register for workshops on GenAI and writing.

What is Generative AI?
Starting with the basics: What GenAI is and why you should learn about it.

How does Generative AI work?
How GenAI works and the sources of data that it is trained on.

GenAI and academic integrity in assessment
The acceptable use of GenAI in UCL assessments.

Writing effective prompts for GenAI
How to write prompts that guide GenAI to give you useful outputs.

Capabilities and limitations of GenAI
When GenAI can be helpful and when it is likely to give less successful results.

Ethics and sustainability of GenAI
The ethical concerns around GenAI services and how sustainable they are.

Safeguarding your data with GenAI
How GenAI services might use your data and how to keep your data safe.

GenAI in work and study
Ideas for everyday study, work and leisure tasks that can incorporate GenAI.
